enzymes.name: CYP3A4
enzymes.description: The most abundant cytochrome P450 enzyme in the liver and intestines, responsible for metabolizing over 50% of clinically prescribed medications.

enzymes.name: CYP2D6
enzymes.description: Responsible for the metabolism of about 25% of clinical drugs, especially neuropsychiatric, cardiovascular, and pain medications. Highly subject to genetic polymorphism.

enzymes.name: CYP2C9
enzymes.description: Responsible for the metabolism of many acidic drugs, including oral anticoagulants, NSAIDs, and oral hypoglycemics.
